text:
An overwhelmedSharron Davieswas unable to hold back her tears as she remembered hermother's gruelling last moments.

More than 30,000 people have developed silent killer infection hepatitis C and HIV after being affected by contaminated blood products and transfusions between 1970 and 1991. Around 3,000 of these individuals passed away during what is now dubbedthe biggest scandalin the history of theNHS.

As Prime MinisterRishi Sunakis expected to issue a public apology this Monday, May 20,Good Morning Britain'sSusanna ReidandRichard Madeleyspoke to guests about the controversy, more notably a woman named Lauren, with Sharron welcomed to intervene via video call.

Speaking about the scandal, Lauren Palmer calmly explained she'd been orphaned at the age of 9 when her parents died from the HIV virus eight days apart, after her father was infected by contaminated blood. She was accompanied This prompted the former Olympic swimmer to open up about her mother's death.

Sharron's mother Sheila developed liver cancer in her forties after being given contaminated blood during an operation and suffered from horrific side effects from the medication she'd been given as part of her treatment. Clearly moved, Sharron branded Lauren's dignified attitude "extraordinary" before almost bursting into tears.

"I had my mum until she was 78, even though it's 20 years, it's quite difficult and she had broken bones and all sorts of things from the drugs she was given", Sharron explained as she choked back tears. "But I had her but these two poor people didn't have their parents and this was ignored."

She continued, urging the British government to take action for those affected by the scandal and recognise the mistake after years of impunity: "other countries managed to get convictions against drug companies. We are the last in theworldto actually sort this problem out - and this is happening scandal after scandal and it must stop! We have to start dealing with our mistakes, and face them, and deal with people who are the victims."

Sharron concluded: "we have to start dealing with out mistakes, face them and look after people", while off-scree, multiple viewers reacted to her claims. ""There is something seriously wrong with our country if as Sharron says we are literally the last country to sort this out. What’s wrong with us that we need so much planning and consultation to sort this out?", one of them notably penned.

The former athlete has frequently shone a light on the injustice her mother suffered after she was originally brought into hospital for an operation to treat gallstones. As mentioned, she was administered contaminated blood which led her to develop hepatitis C. The infection was diagnosed years after her operation during a blood test before considerably damaging her liver and causing cancer.

Sheila drew her last breath in 2017 with Sharron telling the Sunday Times earlier in May, 2024: "my mum suffered for 30 years of her life with it." She added: "It would have been really good if Mum had had some help towards the end because she was a pensioner and she was desperate to leave her house to her children. That was the most important thing she wanted, so she scrimped and saved in the last few years just to make sure that the house was there for us when she died."

title: Olympic legend Sharron Davies in tears over mum's tragic death after being given infected blood - The Mirror
author: Laura Carreno
description: Sharron Davies held back tears this Monday on Good Morning Britain as she spoke to a guest who had lost their father to HIV after he was infected with contaminated blood
keywords: Good Morning Britain, ITV, Sharron Davies
